subject: feral cats
one_liner: A six-figure autonomous electric vehicle for an animal that has never once asked to go anywhere.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan founder sincerity in a luxury-automotive register, applied to creatures with no use for any of it. Spec-sheet confidence about horsepower, range, and torque that the occupant will never engage. Never wink at the camera. The car is real, expensive, and waiting at the curb. The cat is not coming.

hero.h1: The first luxury EV engineered around the feral cat.
hero.sub: 1,034 horsepower. 412 miles of range. A heated cabin your cat will never enter. Prowl is the autonomous electric vehicle built, against all available evidence, for feral cats.

features.title: Subdermal key fob
features.blurb: Feral cats cannot be trusted with a key, so we put the key inside the cat. A grain-sized transponder, implanted between the shoulder blades, unlocks all four doors the instant your cat sprints past the vehicle and continues into the storm drain.

features.title: Destination-free autonomy
features.blurb: Level 4 self-driving, tuned for an occupant with no address, no schedule, and no intention of getting in. Prowl maps every dumpster, crawlspace, and warm hood in a two-mile radius, idles at the curb, recalculates, and idles again.

features.title: Whisker-calibrated suspension
features.blurb: The adaptive air suspension reads ride height off your cat's whiskers, which are not in the vehicle. When the whiskers twitch somewhere across the parking lot, all four corners adjust in eight milliseconds. Nobody is in the car.

pricing_tiers.name: Stray
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the cat who tolerates one human, barely.
perks: One Prowl, in any color the cat ignores
24/7 concierge the cat will never call

pricing_tiers.name: Colony
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the lot behind the laundromat.

pricing_tiers.name: Apex Feral
pricing_tiers.tagline: Purchased outright, the way the cat would want.
perks: The full vehicle, deeded to the cat
Lifetime replacement for tires it claws
Founder's plaque engraved with a name the cat does not have
Track package the cat will not track on

testimonials.quote: We reserved four. The cats have not approached any of them. The cars idle at the curb every morning at six, exactly as promised. It is the most reliable thing in my life.
testimonials.name: Dolores Penhallow
testimonials.title: Trap-Neuter-Return Coordinator, Greater Tacoma Feline Liaison Office

testimonials.quote: I doubted the autonomy could handle an occupant who refuses to occupy. It handles it flawlessly. The car has now driven eleven hundred miles with the cat watching from the fence.
testimonials.name: Garrett Ngo
testimonials.title: Volunteer Colony Caretaker and Retired Marine Surveyor

testimonials.quote: The subdermal fob worked on the first attempt. The cat unlocked the vehicle, made eye contact with me, and left. We are calling it a successful onboarding.
testimonials.name: Marisol Treadwell
testimonials.title: Assistant Director of Outdoor Cat Affairs, Cuyahoga County

demo_screen_concept: A fleet-operations console titled Colony Operations, for a caretaker managing a fleet of Prowls assigned to named feral cats. A live roster pairs each cat to a vehicle and shows battery level, current vehicle status (almost always Idling, occupant unreachable), and last-known cat sighting. A Summon to occupant button dispatches the car and reports back, via toast, that the cat declined. Filter tabs by colony, a sortable roster, and a per-cat detail modal showing telemetry: autonomous miles driven versus miles with the cat aboard, which is always zero.

design_direction.style: retro-futurist concept-car showroom. Dark instrument-cluster luxe: a midnight steel-blue ground, brushed-metal gradient panels, hairline chrome rules, wide tracked-out nameplate display type, and a gauge-cluster motif. Turn-signal amber as the action color against a xenon-headlamp blue glow. CSS-only, no WebGL, performant.
design_direction.archetype: Hero-Centric automotive launch page: full-bleed hero with a wide nameplate headline and a spec ribbon, feature blocks, testimonials, pricing trims, and a demo CTA.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Slow instrument-cluster reveals. Spec numbers count up on scroll, hairline rules draw in left-to-right, a soft xenon glow pulses behind the hero. Everything respects prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Wide uppercase tracked-out nameplate over a brushed-metal panel, a horizontal spec ribbon (hp / range / 0-60 / occupants: 0) in mono figures, amber primary CTA, xenon-blue glow bleeding up from the baseline like headlights at the horizon.
